info.title: easyVerein REST-API documentation
info.description:
This version is the latest version, you can use the alias latest
If you want to switch from API version 2.0 to 3.0 please check out our integrations guide.



General overview

This API provides you with access to data and routines of the easyVerein software and allows you to integrate easyVerein into other tools and services. You may access and update information stored in easyVerein using this API or invoke procedures of this software. You may use this API to build your custom workflows, exchange data with tools or extend the functionality of the software.

Rate limit

Requests are limited to 100/min.

Responsibility

Please read the instructions carefully. Be aware that we cannot take any responsibility when you alter, disclose or delete any data unintentionally. Any API call authenticated with your API token is taken as a data processing instruction of your organization. §3 and §4 of our data processing agreement (AVV) also applies to instructions given by using the API. This also applies when you call API endpoints by using scripts, third-party applications, external tools or other software solutions.

Support

Please note that our customer support team cannot provide you with support for our API services. If you have questions regarding this API that are not covered by our documentation our support team may forward your request to our technical team but please be aware that it might take longer than usual to get a response. Our technical team cannot check or verify your implementations and scripts. We cannot provide you with support for custom software or third-party applications.

Authentication

To use this API you need to transfer a token for every request within the header. To do so you need to set a header variable like this: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <YOUR-API-KEY>'.

Since v2.0 the tokens are valid for 30 days. 15 days after the creation/refresh of a token the token should be refreshed (replaced by a new one) using the refresh-token endpoint

The API will inform you that your token needs to be refreshed by setting the token_refresh_needed attribute in every header of every response. A simple GET request on the refresh-token endpoint will give you a new token that replaces the old token immediately. v2.0 will enforce the use of this token.

Applications

An application is also a normal member but with the is_application flag set to true. To make a new application we strongly recommend to send the applications data to your backend and send them from there to the API using the token of the organization. We also recommend the use of any spam prevention like the reCaptcha.

Good to know

The member- and the contact-details- table needs each other, if you plan to create a user, please do it like this: First create a contact-details (POST to the contact-details endpoint) and enter at least all required fields. The fields that are required depends on the 'is_company' attribute. If it is set to "true", 'company_name' is the only field you have to define. If it is set to "false" (which is the default), you need to define at least the 'first_name' and the 'family_name'. After you have successfully created the contact details, you can create a member. The required fields for the member are: 'email_or_user_name' and 'contact_details'. If you set 'is_application' to "true" you automatically create a membership application which can be manually reviewed within the software. We recommend to do so if you build your own application form. Please note: The relationship of contact_details to member is a composition, the member requires the contact_details, the contact_details can exist without a member.

You can reference to the contact-details-endpoint (and any other relation) in three ways:

  1. With a string, containing the http-reference like the API shows the relation (for example https://easyverein.com/api/v3.0/contact-details/{pk})
  2. With a string, containing the primary key (pk) of the reference
  3. With an integer as the primary key (pk) of the reference

It is highly recommended to use the ?query term if possible. The API will optimize the performance depending on the queried data. So please consider that the API can be faster if you limit the fields to get.

It is also possible to define the amount of entries to view per page using the ?limit term. The default value of it is 5 and the maximum value is 100

What about this bulk-endpoints?

The bulk-update and bulk-create endpoints can include multiple requests for the same endpoint. It is possible to use these endpoints to create or change a big amount of entries in just one single request.

This is much more time saving and even energy efficient than doing a single request per entry.

We recommend to use these endpoints when possible to improve performance, saving energy and time.

Why doesn't each endpoint have a bulk endpoint?

It is theoretically possible to activate this for every endpoint. For now the bulks are just active for:member,contact-details,invoice,event,booking,lending,application-form-element,organization-token,participation-price-group,member-group-assignment,member-custom-field-assignment,contact-details-custom-field-assignment,event-custom-field-assignment,inventory-object-custom-field-assignment,select-option,participation,

We just think that the bulk actions are not usefully for every endpoint.

How to upload a file? (e.g. profile_picture)

Uploading a file isn't possible for POST requests. It is possible to PATCH files though. Uploading with a PATCH just works like a normal PATCH but with a special header set:

'Content-Disposition': 'attachment; name="samplename"; filename="samplepicture.jpg"'

The body of the PATCH includes the key of the field to upload the file for (e.g. profile_picture) and a byte stream as value.

Samples

For further examples on how this API works, please take a look on our REST-API-Samples hosted on GitLab.

There is also an inofficial Node.js library and some actively developed projects (for example a Discord Bot and a custom payment page) on GitHub! Thanks to Fensterbank for sharing his implementations!

The API has also an unofficial (community) python library now!


A good sample for a get request with nested query:

Request:

https://easyverein.com/api/v3.0/member?query={id,contact_details{id,name},org{id,name,short},email_or_user_name,membership_number}

Response:

	
		{
			"membership_number": "",
			"org": {
				"id": ,
				"short": "",
				"name": ""
			},
			"id": ,
			"contact_details": {
				"id": ,
				"name": ""
			},
			"email_or_user_name": ""
		}

Request:

https://easyverein.com/api/v3.0/contact-details?query={id,custom_fields{address_object{custom_fields{address_object{id}}}}}

Response:

	
		{
			"id": ,
			"custom_fields": [{
				"address_object": {
					"custom_fields": "https://easyverein.com/api/v3.0/contact-details//custom-fields/"
				}
			}]
		}

There is a recursion limit for the API to prevent the same entry from being retrieved again in a request.

Request:

https://easyverein.com/api/v3.0/member?query={id,custom_fields{-org,-user_object,-delete_after_date,-deleted_by}}

Response:

	
		{
			"id": ,
			"custom_fields": [{
				"id": ,
				"custom_field": "https://easyverein.com/api/v3.0/custom-field/",
				"value": ""
			}]
		}

It is also possible to define a negative query to exclude some fields from a response, this can be helpfully if a request needs most of the fields but does not need all. This is made possible by putting a '-' in front of the field: ?query={-$field}

Changelog

Changes 13.05.2026:

  • member-custom-field-assignment

    • GET: Added new filters value, value__in, value__ne to filter by value for custom fields that aren't select or multi-select custom fields, and selected_option, selected_option__value to filter by a selected option.

  • contact-details-custom-field-assignment

    • GET: Added new filters value, value__in, value__ne to filter by value for custom fields that aren't select or multi-select custom fields, and selected_option, selected_option__value to filter by a selected option.

  • inventory-object-custom-field-assignment

    • GET: Added new filters value, value__in, value__ne to filter by value for custom fields that aren't select or multi-select custom fields, and selected_option, selected_option__value to filter by a selected option.

  • event-custom-field-assignment

    • GET: Added new filters value, value__in, value__ne to filter by value for custom fields that aren't select or multi-select custom fields, and selected_option, selected_option__value to filter by a selected option.

Changes 07.05.2026:

  • contact-details:

    • GET: Fixed that member_area_member_list_columns is considered for chairman without address-permission.

  • participation:

    • POST: Fixed that the bulk-create-participations-with-price-groups can now create price group participations where several participations have the same price group.

  • chairman-level:

    • PATCH: Fixed that adding or removing other chairmen to/from the own chairmanlevel is possible.
    • PATCH: Fixed that removing the module_account permission from the own chairmanlevel is not possible.

Changes 29.04.2026:

  • participation:

    • POST: Fixed that multiple participations cannot be created anymore in interval events.

Changes 27.04.2026:

  • verify-email:

    • POST: Fixed that this endpoint also uses snake_case.

Changes 22.04.2026:

  • contact-details:

    • GET: Fixed that the filter like_search fully supports snake_case.

  • member-custom-field-assignment:

    • PATCH: Fixed a rare edgecase where the API crashes when patching a field with certain permissions.

Changes 20.04.2026:

  • All:

    • Introduces the new version v3.0 of the easyVerein API.
    • [breaking] Renamed every field into snake_case and removed starting underscores from some fields to be more consistent in the API. For more detailed information on these changes please refer to our integrations guide.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The error messages for every reference field will now be a dict containing its field name as key and a list of errors as value. We plan to change every error message to match this pattern in the future!
    • GET: Removed the deprecated deleted filter. This filter has no use anymore, use the wastebasket endpoint instead.

  • article-object

    • GET: [breaking] The read only field availablePieces will now return -1 instead of the string "unbegrenzt" when there is no limit.
    • GET/PATCH/POST: [breaking] Renamed the fields earningAbsolute and earningPercent to profit_margin_per_unit. and profit_margin_per_unit_in_percent. Both fields are now read only.

  • booking:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter billing_id__isempty to billing_id.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter bookingprojectassignment to booking_project_assignment.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter bookingprojectassignment__not to booking_project_assignment_ne.

  • [New] chairman-tutorial

    • GET: Returns the chairman-tutorial object for the currently authenticated chairman. Only the owning chairman can access their own object.
    • GET/PATCH/POST: New endpoint to track which tutorial sections an admin (chairman) has already seen. Only accessible to chairmen. Each chairman can only have one instance.
    • PATCH: Updates the seen-flags of the tutorial sections. Available fields: tutorial_members_seen, tutorial_events_seen, tutorial_addresses_seen, tutorial_mailing_seen, tutorial_bookkeeping_seen, tutorial_bookkeeping_invoices_seen, tutorial_bookkeeping_statistics_seen, tutorial_bookkeeping_invoices_open_seen.
    • POST: Creates a chairman-tutorial object for the current chairman. Only allowed if no instance exists yet.
    • DELETE: Not allowed.

  • chat-settings:

    • GET: Removed the redundant orgUser field.
    • GET/PATCH: Added new boolean field playSoundOnNewMessage. When true, a notification sound is played when a new message arrives and the user is not active in the affected room. Defaults to true.

  • contact-details:

    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint contact-details/{address_id}/custom-fields was replaced by a new main endpoint contact-details-custom-field-assignment. To filter by address_id you can use ?address_object={address_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint contact-details/{address_id}/change-requests was replaced by a new main endpoint contact-details-change-request. To filter by address_id you can use ?address={address_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the field geoPositionCoords to geo_position_coordinates.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter is_referenced_by_org_user to is_referenced_by_member.
    • PATCH: Setting the _preferredEmailField to a field without a valid email in it is no longer allowed if the contact-details are from a _isChairman=true member.
    • PATCH/POST: To remove all group assignments, contact_details_groups needs to be set to the empty list [] instead of null.

  • [New] contact-details-change-request:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the contact-details/{address_id}/custom-fields sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.

  • [New] contact-details-custom-field-assignment:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the contact-details/{address_id}/custom-fields sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • PATCH/POST: The field value does no longer tolerate true as value. It has to be a string now!

  • contact-details-log:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The field value does no longer support boolean values. It always has to be a string now.

  • custom-field:

    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint custom-field/{custom_field_id}/select-options was replaced by a new main endpoint select-option. To filter by custom_field_id you can use ?custom_field={custom_field_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.

  • [New] debit-order

    • GET: New endpoint to retrieve direct-debit orders.

  • event:

    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint event/{event_id}/custom-fields was replaced by a new main endpoint event-custom-field-assignment. To filter by event_id you can use ?event_object={event_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint event/{event_id}/participation was replaced by a new main endpoint participation. To filter by event_id you can use ?participation_event={event_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter location_object__not to location_object__ne.
    • POST: [breaking] Replaced the value usePaymentInformationFromReceiver for payment_information in action generate-invoices to use_payment_information_from_receiver.
    • POST: [breaking] The invite-groups action that was previously on this endpoint has been moved to the new participation endpoint, and it requires the event to be given as participation_event.

  • [New] event-custom-field-assignment:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the event/{event_id}/custom-fields sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The field value does no longer support boolean values. It always has to be a string now.

  • feature-request:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the action voteFor to vote-for.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the action voteAgainst to vote-against.

  • inventory-object:

    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint inventory-object/{inventory_object_id}/custom-fields was replaced by a new main endpoint inventory-object-custom-field-assignment. To filter by inventory_object_id you can use ?inventory_object={inventory_object_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET: [breaking] Returning all lendings now on the lendings field instead of only all lendings that do not have the state returned and inquiry. This is because the field name does not imply this behaviour and would be misleading.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter location_object__not to location_object__ne.
    • PATCH/POST: To remove all group assignments, inventory_object_groups needs to be set to the empty list [] instead of null.

  • [New] inventory-object-custom-field-assignment:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the inventory-object/{inventory_object_id}/custom-fields sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The field value does no longer support boolean values. It always has to be a string now.

  • invoice:

    • ALL: Fix issue that invoice of mode offer_template could not be saved without any positions.
    • GET: Added show_bookable filter (in combination with direct_debit_order) to return only currently bookable invoices for assignment.
    • GET: The field custom_payment_method is now a real reference field instead of just an ID.
    • PATCH/POST: Fixed the issue that invoices with mode offer_template could not be saved without any positions.

  • invoice-item:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter related_invoice__not to related_invoice__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter billing_account__not to billing_account__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET: The field custom_payment_method is now a real reference field instead of just an ID.

  • lending:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter parent_inventory_object__not to parent_inventory_object__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter borrow_address__not to borrow_address__ne.

  • location:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the field geoPositionCoords to geo_position_coordinates.

  • member:

    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint member/{member_id}/groups was replaced by a new main endpoint member-group-assignment. To filter by member_id you can use ?user_object={member_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint member/{member_id}/custom-fields was replaced by a new main endpoint member-custom-field-assignment. To filter by member_id you can use ?user_object={member_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • ALL: [breaking] The sub endpoint member/{member_id}/custom-fields/{custom_field_assignment_id}/change-requests was replaced by a new main endpoint member-custom-field-assignment-change-request. To filter by custom_field_assignment_id you can use ?user_custom_field={custom_field_assignment_id}. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter chairman_permission_group__not to chairman_permission_group__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter has_copy_in_org__not to has_copy_in_org__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter application_kind__not to application_kind__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET/PATCH/POST: Removed the unnecessary field applicationKind. Use the application_form reference instead to get the formular_kind.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed parameter smtpId to smtp_email_setting_id in action email-notification.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed parameter authToken to access_token in action me/get-ev-community-token.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed the action me/register-twoFA to me/register-two-fa.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed the action me/deactivate-twoFA to me/deactivate-two-fa.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed the action deactivate-twoFA to deactivate-two-fa.
    • POST: member/me/register-two-fa now returns a json payload with keys message and device with status code 200 if the request was successful.

  • [New] member-custom-field-assignment:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the member/{member_id}/custom-fields sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The field value does no longer support boolean values. It always has to be a string now.

  • [New] member-custom-field-assignment-change-request:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the member/{member_id}/custom-fields/{custom_field_assignment_id}/change-requests sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The field field_value does no longer support boolean values. It always has to be a string now.
    • POST: The fields field_value and selected_options are no longer mandatory.

  • member-group:

    • POST: [breaking] Renamed action setPaymentActive to set-payment-active.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed action overwriteInterval to overwrite-interval.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed action overwriteAmount to overwrite-amount.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed action overwritePaymentStartDate to overwrite-payment-start-date.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed action setDosbSports to set-dosb-sports.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed action removeDosbSports to remove-dosb-sports.

  • [New] member-group-assignment:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the member/{member_id}/groups sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter user_object__not zo user_object__ne.
    • PATCH/POST: [breaking] The field value does no longer support boolean values. It always has to be a string now.
    • POST [New]: Added the action recalculate-group-change-dates to recalculate member group assignment dates. Caution: This action will rewrite existing start/end dates, also in the past!

  • organization

    • GET: The trend action now accepts start and end as explicit date parameters, replacing the previous fixed interval of months relative to the current date.
    • GET: Changed the default value of the fields default_profile_picture, member_area_title_picture and bookkeeping_donation_signing_image to empty strings instead of null.
    • GET: The field child_organizations now needs to be referenced in the query as child_organizations{*} to see its attributes.
    • GET [New]: Added the action revenue-preview to retrieve membership fee revenue preview for the next 12 months.

  • organization-settings:

    • GET/PATCH: [breaking] Renamed the field appNotificationMemberCustomFieldRequesRefused to appNotificationMemberCustomFieldRequestRefused fixing a typo.

  • [New] participation:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the event/{event_id}/participation sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter participation_address__not to participation_address__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter participation_event__not to participation_event__ne.
    • POST: [breaking] The invite action now requires a list of addresses participation_addresses instead of a single address participation_address.
    • POST: [breaking] The invite-groups action that was previously on the event endpoint has been moved to the participation endpoint and it requires the event to be given as participation_event.

  • participation-price-group:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter price_group__not to price_group__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter participation__not to participation__ne

  • post:

    • GET: [breaking] Returning null instead of the string "Gelöschter Benutzer" when the poster is no longer in the organization.
    • GET: [breaking] The field org was removed, use topic{forum{org}} in the query instead.

  • protocol:

    • GET: [breaking] Removed the fields parentProtocolElements and publicProtocolUploads because they are the same as protocol_elements and protocol_uploads but with filters. Use the associated endpoints of those references to filter their entries.

  • [New] public-chat-room:

    • GET: List all public chat rooms in the organization. Supports search (by name, topic, room_id), ordering (by id, name, created_at), and pagination.
    • GET: Retrieve a specific public room by its Matrix room_id (e.g., !abc123:server.com).
    • PATCH: Update public room metadata (name, topic) for registered rooms (chairman only).
    • POST: Register a Matrix room as public within the organization (chairman only). Creates new or updates existing room registration. Note: This doesn't imply a registration in the matrix server! New entries in public-chat-room will not cause a creation of a matrix chat room!
    • DELETE: Unregister a room from the organization's public rooms list (chairman only). Does not delete from Matrix server.
    • DELETE: New action delete-from-matrix to permanently delete a room from both the organization list AND the Matrix server via admin service (chairman only).

  • price-group:

    • POST: [breaking] Renamed the value usePaymentInformationFromReceiver for payment_information in action generate-invoices to use_payment_information_from_receiver.

  • protocol-element-comment:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter protocol_element__not to protocol_element__ne.

  • [New] select-option:

    • ALL: This endpoint replaces the custom-field/{custom_field_id}/select-options sub endpoint. For more information on filters, required fields and more please refer to our integrations guide.

  • smtp-email-setting:

    • POST: [breaking] Renamed parameter _smtpPassword to smtp_password in action update-password.

  • task

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter usesessionfilter to use_session_filter.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter parent_event__not to parent_event__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter task_group__not to task_group__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter protocol_element__not to protocol_element__ne.
    • POST: [breaking] Renamed parameter smtpId to smtp_email_setting_id in action send-email.

  • task-comment:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter parent_task__not to parent_task__ne.
    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter author__not to author__ne. Fixed the author filter in general.

  • topic:

    • GET: [breaking] Returning null instead of the string "Gelöschter Benutzer" when the poster is no longer in the organization.
    • GET: [breaking] The field org was removed, use forum{org} in the query instead.

  • voting:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter allowed_member_groups__not to allowed_member_groups__ne.

  • voting-question:

    • GET: [breaking] Renamed the filter voting__not to voting__ne.



Available API endpoints

Browse all available endpoints below. Click Authorize and enter your API token to authenticate. Your token will be applied to all requests made from this page. To send a request, open an endpoint and click Try it out.

Warning: Requests executed via Try it out are real API calls against your live organization data. Any changes you make — including creating, updating or deleting records — will take effect immediately.

name.description: Zeichenwert; Der Name Der Abstimmung; Maximal 500 Zeichen

start.description: Zeit Und Datumswert; Zeitpunkt An Dem Die Abstimmung Startet

end.description: Zeit Und Datumswert; Zeitpunkt An Dem Die Abstimmung Beendet Ist

allow_abstentions.description: Boolscher Wert; Enthaltungen Erlauben (Leere Abstimmung Abgeben Möglich); Standardwert: True

access.description: Auswahl; Repräsentation Des Zugangsstatuses In Form Einer Zahl; Standardwert: 0; Zusatz: Möglich Ist: 0 -> Nur Eingeloggte Mitglieder, 1 -> Öffentlicher Link, 2 -> Individueller Link

member_visibility.description: Auswahl; Repräsentationm Der Sichtbarkeit Des Ergebnisses Für Mitglieder In Form Einer Zahl; Standardwert: 0; Zusatz: Möglich Ist: 0 -> Kein Ergebnis, 1 -> Endergebnis Für Alle Teilnahmeberechtigten, 2 -> Vorläufiges Ergebnis Nach Teilnahme, 3 -> Vorläufiges Ergebnis Vor Teilnahme

admin_visibility.description: Auswahl; Repräsentation Der Sichtbarkeit Des Ergebnisses Für Admins In Form Einer Zahl (Admins Sehen Mindestens So Viel Wie Normale Mitglieder); Standardwert: 0; Zusatz: Möglich Ist: 0 -> Endergebnis, 1 -> Vorläufiges Ergebnis Nach Teilnahme, 2 -> Vorläufiges Ergebnis Vor Teilnahme

voting_mode.description: Auswahl; Repräsentation Des Moduses Für Abstimmungen In Form Einer Zahl; Standardwert: 0; Zusatz: Möglich Ist: 0 -> Geheime Abstimmung, 1 -> Teilnahmen Anzeigen, 2 -> Ergebnis Komplett Sichtbar

submission_note.description: Zeichenwert; Ein Kurzer Text Welcher Nach Der Teilnahme Erscheinen Soll; Maximal 300 Zeichen; Standardwert: 'Vielen Dank Für Deine Stimme'

get.summary:
Get the new token.
Every new token is expiresIn days valid (usually 30 days) but should be replaced after half the valid time.
--
If a token is only valid for 10 minutes or less, we tell the client to immediately refresh the token.

get.summary: Get the currently set session filters

401.description: Unauthorized

500.description: Internal server error

post.summary: Set one or more session filters

requestBody.description: The body containing the filters that can be changed/set

items.description: A rule of the filter, rules can also contain other rules (recursion)

id.description: The field id the filter is pointing at. If it is a field from another endpoint the relatedName and the fieldName are seperated by two _. For example: `contactDetails__state`

field.description: The field the filter is pointing at. Basically the same as the id

type.description: The type of the fields value. For example: `double` or `string` or `date`

input.description: The type of the input in the UI, its not really usefully in the API but for the UI. For example: `text`

operator.description: The operator for the filter. Negating a filter with the same operator but with `not_` at the start of the operator. For example: `equal`, `not_equal`, `greater`, `less`, `contains`, `is_null`. The `is_null` operator does not have a negative counterpart for obvious reasons

value.description: The value of the field to filter for

requestBody.description: The body containing the properties that can be changed/set

username.description: The username or email address of the user that is also used to log in

password.description: The password of the user to authenticate

2FA.description: The two factor authentication token (only required if the user trying to login has 2fa enabled)

post.description: Getting the token for the user to authenticate himself to use the api. This endpoint needs the login data of the user. Please consider, that `username` is build like this: `$orgShort_$emailOrUsername`. So if your orgshort is for example "abc" and your username is "some@example.de" the username string to use for authentication is `abc_some@example.de`.
post.summary: Get the token for a user to authenticate

200.description: If "needs2FA" in the response body is true it is required, otherwise the response body contains the token

400.description: The login credentials are not valid

requestBody.description: The body containing the properties that can be changed/set

email.description: The email address to send the password reset email

short.description: The short of the organisation

post.description: Triggering the password reset mechanism by sending out a password-reset email to the given email address
post.summary: Triggering the password reset mechanism

204.description: The reset password email was send successfully

400.description: One of the required fields is missing or the email does not match any user
